perturbation.original_text: THIS NON-DISCLOSURE/NON-COMPETE AGREEMENT (this “Agreement”) is made this 28th day of February, 2014 by and between TROY HOLDINGS INTERNATIONAL, INC., an Ontario Canada corporation (the “Seller”), EPAZZ, INC., an Illinois corporation (the “Purchaser”), TELECORP PRODUCTS, INC., a Michigan corporation (the “Company”), Troy Inc. (“Shareholder”), ANA MISRA, an individual (“Misra”) and SCOTT MacCANNELL, an individual (“MacCannell”) (Misra and MacCannell are also hereinafter referred to as “Key Personnel”).
perturbation.changed_text: THIS NON-DISCLOSURE/NON-COMPETE AGREEMENT (this “Agreement”) is made this 28th day of February, 2014 by and between TROY HOLDINGS INTERNATIONAL, INC., an Ontario Canada corporation (the “Vendor”), EPAZZ, INC., an Illinois corporation (the “Client”), TELECORP PRODUCTS, INC., a Michigan corporation (the “Company”), Troy Inc. (“Shareholder”), ANA MISRA, an individual (“Misra”) and SCOTT MacCANNELL, an individual (“MacCannell”) (Misra and MacCannell are also hereinafter referred to as “Key Personnel”).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Seller' to 'Vendor' and 'Purchaser' to 'Client' introduces ambiguity. Later sections refer to 'Seller' and 'Purchaser,' creating confusion if 'Vendor' and 'Client' are distinct entities, contradicting the original intention. This change is made in the introduction section, and the places where the 'Seller' and 'Purchaser' are referenced will be contradicted with this change. This is an instance of misaligned terminology
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a material part of the consideration given and received by the parties in connection with the SPA: a. Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder, jointly and severally, acknowledge and agree that in the course of ownership and/or employment with the Company that Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder have acquired and/or the Company has and will continue to provide Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder with, or access to information regarding the business, procedures, activities and services of the Company, including but not limited to, memorandum, files, forms, techniques, methods and procedures, programs, customer accounts and customer lists, supplier lists, costs and prices of the Company, and customer needs, requirements and business affairs (hereinafter referred to collectively as the “Proprietary Property”) as is necessary or desirable to assist him in his activities on behalf of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Seller' to 'Vendor' and 'Purchaser' to 'Client' creates a contradiction because the contradicted text explicitly refers to 'Seller' and 'Purchaser'. If 'Vendor' and 'Client' are considered distinct from 'Seller' and 'Purchaser,' this directly contradicts the obligations and agreements outlined in the contradicted section. This creates a misaligned terminology in the document and ambiguity, fitting the given definition.

perturbation.original_text: Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder, jointly and severally, acknowledge and agree that in the course of ownership and/or employment with the Company that Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder have acquired and/or the Company has and will continue to provide Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder with, or access to information regarding the business, procedures, activities and services of the Company, including but not limited to, memorandum, files, forms, techniques, methods and procedures, programs, customer accounts and customer lists, supplier lists, costs and prices of the Company, and customer needs, requirements and business affairs (hereinafter referred to collectively as the “Proprietary Property”) as is necessary or desirable to assist him in his activities on behalf of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor, Key Personnel and Shareholder, jointly and severally, acknowledge and agree that in the course of ownership and/or employment with the Company that Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder have acquired and/or the Company has and will continue to provide Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder with, or access to information regarding the business, procedures, activities and services of the Company, including but not limited to, memorandum, files, forms, techniques, methods and procedures, programs, customer accounts and customer lists, supplier lists, costs and prices of the Company, and customer needs, requirements and business affairs (hereinafter referred to collectively as the “Proprietary Property”) as is necessary or desirable to assist him in his activities on behalf of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Seller' with 'Vendor' in this section creates a contradiction. The sentence now refers to both 'Vendor' and 'Seller,' implying they are different entities, which is not defined and causes in-text contradiction within the same sentence. This creates confusion about who is bound by the non-disclosure agreement. This pertubation is creating an in-text contradiction in section 2a, specifically within the same sentence in this section. This is an instance of misaligned terminology
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder, jointly and severally, acknowledge and agree that in the course of ownership and/or employment with the Company that Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder have acquired and/or the Company has and will continue to provide Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder with, or access to information regarding the business, procedures, activities and services of the Company, including but not limited to, memorandum, files, forms, techniques, methods and procedures, programs, customer accounts and customer lists, supplier lists, costs and prices of the Company, and customer needs, requirements and business affairs (hereinafter referred to collectively as the “Proprietary Property”) as is necessary or desirable to assist him in his activities on behalf of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Seller' with 'Vendor' while the contradicted text refers to 'Seller'. This introduces misaligned terminology by using 'Vendor' and 'Seller' within the same sentence, implying they are different entities without defining them, causing a direct conflict and contradiction within the contract as it creates ambiguity regarding who is bound by the agreement. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: If any party commences an action against another party to enforce any of the terms, covenants, conditions or provisions of this Agreement, or because of a breach by a party of its obligations under this Agreement, the prevailing party in any such action shall be entitled to recover its losses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees, costs and interest incurred in connection with the enforcement of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: If any party commences an action against another party to enforce any of the terms, covenants, conditions or provisions of this Agreement, or because of a breach by a party of its responsibilities under this Agreement, the prevailing party in any such action shall be entitled to recover its losses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees, costs and interest incurred in connection with the enforcement of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'obligations' to 'responsibilities' is subtle but introduces a potential conflict. 'Obligations' are legally binding duties, while 'responsibilities' can be broader and include moral or ethical duties. If a party breaches a responsibility that isn't a legally defined obligation, it's unclear if the prevailing party costs clause applies. This pertubation is creating an in-text contradiction in section 14, specifically within the same sentence in this section. This is an instance of misaligned terminology
perturbation.contradicted_text: If any party commences an action against another party to enforce any of the terms, covenants, conditions or provisions of this Agreement, or because of a breach by a party of its responsibilities under this Agreement, the prevailing party in any such action shall be entitled to recover its losses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees, costs and interest incurred in connection with the enforcement of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'obligations' to 'responsibilities' creates a contradiction because 'obligations' are legally binding, while 'responsibilities' can be broader. The original text links the prevailing party clause specifically to a breach of 'obligations'. The changed text, also found in the contradicted text section, introduces 'responsibilities', which are not necessarily legally binding obligations. This creates uncertainty as to whether the prevailing party clause applies to a breach of a 'responsibility' that is not a legally defined 'obligation', thus a strong in-text contradiction according to the Misaligned Terminology definition.
